name: Grafana
what: Visualization and observability UI platform for dashboards, alerts, logs, and multiple data sources.
solves: Operational dashboards, metrics visualization, logs/traces integration, and shared visibility across technical systems.
fit: Strong fit as the main observability UI once Prometheus and other telemetry sources exist.
runtime: Web application with internal metadata DB and connectors to data sources like Prometheus, Loki, InfluxDB, PostgreSQL, etc.
components: UI, datasource plugins, alerting, dashboards, auth, and metadata store.
deploy: Straightforward self-hosting; usually paired with Prometheus and exporters.
deps: Metadata storage, data-source connectors, auth/SSO, reverse proxy, and alerting config.
datastores: Internal metadata store plus external data sources.
auth: Local auth and SSO/OIDC/SAML options depending on edition/setup.
apis: Dashboard and datasource APIs, plugins, and alerting APIs.
security: Dashboards can expose internal service data. Use RBAC and least-privilege datasource access.
setup_path: Deploy with Prometheus and exporters as part of the observability wave.
env: Admin creds, datasource settings, auth/SSO, SMTP, and storage path.
persistence: Back up Grafana config/DB or provision dashboards as code.
operations: Datasource governance, dashboard sprawl control, and version/plugin upgrades matter.
failure_points: Letting Grafana become a manual dashboard graveyard without provisioning discipline.
integration: Natural fit with Prometheus, Postgres exporters, Kong, Redis, and infrastructure monitoring.
verify: Choose whether dashboards are provisioned as code from day one.
gdpr: Avoid exposing identifiable data in dashboards unless role-controlled.
overlap: Complements rather than replaces Prometheus/Uptime Kuma.
codex_read_first: Read observability target list and decide on dashboards-as-code.
codex_best_path: Use Grafana as the shared observability UI over Prometheus and selected app/data sources.
codex_never_assume: Never connect Grafana to production sources with broad privileges without RBAC planning.
